body.title: Granny: route audit-fail tickets to CC inbox, not silent skip
body.result: _hold_for_audit_fail already implemented in daemon.py (blocks ticket + alerts CC.0 + posts GRANNY_HOLD_AUDIT_FAIL); test coverage exists at test_run_once_alerts_on_audit_fail; no code changes needed
body.description:
When a ticket fails Granny's audit (missing description sections, invalid size, etc.), Granny currently logs a warning and posts to channel, but the ticket stays in sprint and will fail audit again on every poll cycle. CC never sees it to fix it.

Proposed shape: on audit fail, put the ticket on hold with the audit reasons as the hold message (same pattern as HIGH-inertia hold), and alert CC.0 via IMAP with kind='audit_fail' and the reasons list. CC reviews, fixes the ticket description via cc_queue.py update, then sets back to sprint. Channel post: GRANNY_HOLD_AUDIT_FAIL|ticket=<id>|reasons=<...>.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py (run_once audit-fail branch), tests/test_granny_daemon.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; log every state change; alert deduplication (existing _alert_cc dedup key)
**Scope boundary:** IN: audit-fail → hold + CC.0 alert + GRANNY_HOLD_AUDIT_FAIL channel post. OUT: auto-fixing ticket descriptions (CC does that manually), retry logic after fix.
**Completion criteria:** A ticket with missing Completion criteria gets blocked (not left in sprint), GRANNY_HOLD_AUDIT_FAIL appears in channel, CC.0 IMAP has the alert. Test covering the new path.
